10.desc: You may direct your magic to absorb damage while your Bladesong is active. Whenever you take damage, you may use your reaction to expend 1 spell slot and reduce that damage to you by an amount equal to 5 times the spell slot's level.

14.name: Song of Victory
14.desc: You may add your INT (minimum of +1) to the damage of your melee weapon attacks while your Bladesong is active.

chronurgy-magic.name: Chronurgy Magic
desc: Focusing on the manipulation of time, those who follow the Chronurgy tradition learn to alter the pace of reality to their liking. Using the ramping of anticipatory dunamis energy, these mages can bend the flow of time as adroitly as a skilled musician plays an instrument, lending themselves and their allies an advantage in the blink of an eye.

2.name: Chronal Shift
2.desc: As a reaction, after you or a creature you can see within 30ft. of you makes an attack roll, ability check or saving throw, you may force the creature to reroll. You may make the decision after you see whether the roll succeeds. The target must use the result of the second roll. You may use this ability twice per long rest.

6.name: Temporal Awareness
6.desc: You may add your proficiency bonus to your initiative rolls.

10.name: Arcane Abeyance
10.desc: Whenever you cast a spell using a spell slot of 4th level or lower, you may condense the spell's magic into a mote. The spell is frozen in time at the moment of casting, and held within a grey bead for 1 hour. This bead is a Tiny object, with AC 15 and 1 hitpoint, and is immune to @poison and @psychic damage. When the duration ends, or the bead is destroyed, it vanishes in a flash of light, and the spell is lost. A creature holding the bead can use its action to release the spell within, whereupon the bead disappears. The spell uses your spell attack bonus or save DC, and the spell treats the creature who released it as the caster for all other purposes. You may create a bead once per short rest.

14.name: Convergent Future
14.desc: You may peer through possible futures and magically pull one of them into events around you, ensuring a particular outcome. Whenever you or a creature you can see within 60ft. of you makes an attack roll, ability check or saving throw, you may use your reaction to ignore the die roll and decide whether the number rolled is the minimum needed to succeed, or one less than that number (your choice).

school-of-abjuration.name: School of Abjuration
desc: The School of Abjuration emphasizes magic that blocks, banishes, or protects. Detractors of this school say that its tradition is about denial, negation rather than positive assertion. You understand, however, that ending harmful effects, protecting the weak, and banishing evil influences is anything but a philosophical void. It is a proud and respected vocation. Called abjurers, members of this school are sought when baleful spirits require exorcism, when important locations must be guarded against magical spying, and when portals to other planes of existence must be closed.

2.name: Abjuration Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a abjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Arcane Ward
2.desc: You may weave magic around yourself for protection. Whenever you cast an abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, you can simultaneously use a strand of the spell's magic to create a magical ward on yourself that lasts until you finish a long rest. The ward has hitpoints equal to 2 times your wizard level + INT. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 hitpoints, you take any remaining damage. While the ward has 0 hitpoints, it cannot absorb damage, but its magic remains; whenever you cast an abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, the ward regains a number of hitpoints equal to 2 times the spell's level. You can create a ward once every long rest.

6.name: Projected Ward
6.desc: Whenever a creature that you can see within 30ft. takes damage, you may use your reaction to cause your ward to absorb that damage. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 hitpoints, the warded creature takes the remaining damage.

10.name: Improved Abjuration
10.desc: Whenever you cast an abjuration spell that requires you to make an ability check as a part of casting that spell, you may add your proficiency bonus to that ability check.

14.name: Spell Resistance
14.desc: You gain advantage on saving throws against spells, and gain resistance to the damage of spells.

school-of-conjuration.name: School of Conjuration
desc: As a conjurer, you favor spells that produce objects and creatures out of thin air. You can conjure billowing clouds of killing fog or summon creatures from elsewhere to fight on your behalf. As your mastery grows, you learn spells of transportation and can teleport yourself across vast distances, even to other planes of existence, in an instant.

2.name: Conjuration Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a conjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Minor Conjuration
2.desc: You may use your action to conjure up an inanimate object in your hand, or on the ground in an unoccupied space that you can see, within 10ft. of you. This object can be no larger than 3ft. on a side, and weigh no more than 10lbs., and its form must be that of a nonmagical object that you have seen. The object is visibly magical, radiating dim light out to 5ft. The object disappears after 1 hour, when you use this feature again, or if it takes or deals any damage.

6.name: Benign Transportation
6.desc: You may use your action to teleport up to 30ft. to an unoccupied space that you can see. Alternatively, you can choose a space within range that is occupied by a Small or Medium creature. If that creature is willing, you both teleport, swapping places. You may use this feature once per long rest. You regain the use of this feature after casting a conjuration spell of 1st level or higher.

10.name: Focused Conjuration
10.desc: Your concentration cannot be broken as a result of taking damage while concentrating on a conjuration spell.

14.name: Durable Summons
14.desc: Any creature that you summon or create with a conjuration spell has 30 temporary hitpoints.

school-of-divination.name: School of Divination
desc: The counsel of a diviner is sought by royalty and commoners alike, for all seek a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future. As a diviner, you strive to part the veils of space, time, and consciousness so that you can see clearly. You work to master spells of discernment, remote viewing, supernatural knowledge, and foresight.

2.name: Divination Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a divination spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Portent
2.desc: Glimpses of the future begin to press in on your awareness. Whenever you finish a long rest, roll 2d20 and record the numbers rolled. You may replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by you or a creature that you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you may replace a roll in this way only once per turn. Each roll may be used only once, and you lose any unused fortelling rolls upon gaining new ones.

6.name: Expert Divination
6.desc: Casting divination spells becomes so easily to you that it expends only a fraction of your spellcasting efforts. Whenever you cast a divination spell of 2nd level or higher using a spell slot, you regain one expended spell slot. This slot must be of a lower level than the spell, and cannot be higher than 5th level.

10.name: The Third Eye
10.desc: As an action, you may increase your powers of perception. Whenever you do so, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you are incapacited or until a short rest. You may use this feature once per short rest.

content.name: Darkvision
content.desc: You gain 60ft. darkvision.

content.name: Ethereal Sight
content.desc: You are able to see into the Ethereal Plane up to 60ft.

content.name: Greater Comprehension
content.desc: You are able to read any language.

content.name: See Invisibility
content.desc: You are able to see invisible creatures and objects within 10ft. of you that are within line of sight.

14.name: Greater Portent
14.desc: The visions in your dreams intensify and paint a more accurate picture in your mind of what is to come. You may roll 3d20 for your portent feature.

school-of-enchantment.name: School of Enchantment
desc: As a member of the School of Enchantment, you have honed your ability to magically entrance and beguile other people and monsters. Some enchanters are peacemakers who bewitch the violent to lay down their arms and charm the cruel into showing mercy. Others are tyrants who magically bind the unwilling into their service. Most enchanters fall somewhere in between.

2.name: Enchantment Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a enchantment spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Hypnotic Gaze
2.desc: Your soft words and enchanting gaze may magically enthrall another creature. As an action, choose a creature that you can see within 5ft. of you. If the target is able to see or hear you, it must succeed on a WIS saving throw or be charmed by you until the end of your next turn. The charmed creature's speed drops to 0, and the creature is incapacitated and visibly dazed. On subsequent turns, as an action, you are able to maintain this effect, extending its duration until the end of your next turn. The effect ends if you move more than 5ft. away from the target, if the target is not able to hear or see you, or if the target takes damage. Once the effect ends, or if the creature succeeds on its initial saving throw, the creature is immune to this feature until you finish a long rest.

6.name: Instinctive Charm
6.desc: Whenever a creature you can see within 30ft. of you makes an attack roll against you, as a reaction, you may divert the attack, provided that another creature is within the attack's range. You must choose to use this feature before knowing whether the attack hits or misses. Creature that are immune to being charmed are also immune to this feature. The attacker must succeed on a WIS saving throw or target the creature, other than you or itself, that is closest to it. If multiple creatures are closest, the attacker may choose who to target. On a successful save, the target becomes immune to this feature until you finish a long rest.

10.name: Split Enchantment
10.desc: Whenever you cast an enchantmnet spell of 1st level or higher that targets only one creature, you may have it target a second creature.

14.name: Alter Memories
14.desc: You gain the ability to make a creature unaware of your magical influence on it. Whenever you cast an enchantment spell to charm or more creature, you may alter one creature's understanding so that it remains unaware of being charmed. Additionally, once before the spell ends, you may use your action to try and make the chosen creature forget some of the time it spent charmed. The target must succeed on an INT saving throw or lose a number of hours of its memories equal to 1 + CHA (minimum of 1). You may make the creature forget less time, and the amount of time cannot exceed the duration of your enchantment spell.

school-of-evocation.name: School of Evocation
desc: You focus your study on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid. Some evokers find employment in military forces, serving as artillery to blast enemy armies from afar. Others use their spectacular power to protect the weak, while some seek their own gain as bandits, adventurers, or aspiring tyrants.

2.name: Evocation Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a evocation spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Sculpt Spells
2.desc: You are able to create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your evocation spells. Whenever you cast an evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you may choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

6.name: Potent Cantrip
6.desc: Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. Whenever a creature succeeds on a saving throw against your cantrip, the creature takes half the cantrip's damage, but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.

10.name: Empowered Evocation
10.desc: You may add your INT to a damage roll of any evocation spell that you cast (minimum of 1).

14.name: Overchannel
14.desc: You may increase the power of your simpler spells. Whenever you cast a non-cantrip wizard spell of 5th level or lower that deals damage, you may deal maximum damage with that spell. If you use this feature more than once per long rest, you take 2d12 @necrotic damage for each level of the spell, immediately after you cast it. This damage increases by 1d12 per level for each usage of this feature past the second, and cannot be reduced by resistance and immunity.

school-of-illusion.name: School of Illusion
desc: You focus your studies on magic that dazzles the senses, befuddles the mind, and tricks even the wisest folk. Your magic is subtle, but the illusions crafted by your keen mind make the impossible seem real. Some illusionists - including many gnome wizards - are benign tricksters who use their spells to entertain. Others are more sinister masters of deception, using their illusions to frighten and fool others for their personal gain.

2.name: Illusion Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a illusion spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Improved Minor Illusion
2.desc: You learn the @minor illusion cantrip. If you already know this cantrip, you learn a different wizard cantrip of your choice. This cantrip does not count against the number of cantrips known. Whenever you cast @minor illusion, you are able to create both a sound and an image with a single casting.

6.name: Malleable Illusions
6.desc: Whenever you cast an illusion spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, as an action, you may change the nature of that illusion (using the spell's normal parameters for the illusion), provided that you can see the illusion.

10.name: Illusory Self
10.desc: You may create an illusory duplicate of yourself as an instant, almost instinctual reaction to danger. As a reaction when a creature makes an attack roll against you, you may interpose the illusory duplicate between the attacker and yourself. The attack automatically misses you, at which point the illusion dissipates. You may use this feature once per short rest.

14.name: Illusory Reality
14.desc: You have learned the secret of weaving shadow magic into your illusions to give them a semi-reality. Whenever you cast an illusion spell of 1st level or higher, you may choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of the illusion and make that object real. You may do this on your turn as a bonus action while the spell is ongoing. The object remains real for 1 minute. For example, you may create an illusion of a bridge over a chasm and then make it real long enough for your allies to cross. The object cannot deal damage or otherwise directly harm anyone.

school-of-necromancy.name: School of Necromancy
desc: The School of Necromancy explores the cosmic forces of life, death, and undeath. As you focus your studies in this tradition, you learn to manipulate the energy that animates all living things. As you progress, you learn to sap the life force from a creature as your magic destroys its body, transforming that vital energy into magical power you can manipulate. Most people see necromancers as menacing, or even villainous, due to the close association with death. Not all necromancers are evil, but the forces they manipulate are considered taboo by many societies.

2.name: Necromancy Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a necromancy spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Grim Harvest
2.desc: Once per turn, when you kill one or more creatures with a spell of 1st level or higher, you regain hitpoints equal to 2 times the spell's level, or 3 times if it was a necromancy spell. You don't gain this benefit for killing constructs or undead.

6.name: Undead Thralls
6.desc: You may add the @animate dead spell to your spellbook if it is not there already. Whenever you cast @animate dead, you may target one additional corpse or pile of bones, creating another zombie or skeleton as appropriate. Additonally, whenever you create an undead using a necromancy spell, the creature's hitpoint maximum is increased by an amount equal to your wizard level, and the creature may add your proficiency bonus to its weapon damage rolls.

10.name: Inured to Undeath
10.desc: You gain resistance to necrotic damage, and your hitpoint maximum cannot be reduced.

14.name: Command Undead
14.desc: As an action, you may choose 1 undead that you can see within 60ft. of you. That creature must succeed on a CHA saving throw against your wizard spell save DC. On a failure, it becomes friendly to you, and obeys your commands until you use this feature again. On a success, you cannot use this feature on that creature again. Intelligent undead are harder to control in this way. If the target has an INT score of 8 or higher, it gains advantage on the saving throw. If the target has an INT score of 12 or higher, it may repeat the saving throw at the end of every hour, ending the effect on itself on a success.

school-of-transmutation.name: School of Transmutation
desc: You are a student of spells that modify energy and matter. To you, the world is not a fixed thing, but eminently mutable, and you delight in being an agent of change. You wield the raw stuff of creation and learn to alter both physical forms and mental qualities. Your magic gives you the tools to become a smith on reality's forge.

Some transmuters are tinkerers and pranksters, turning people into toads and transforming copper into silver for fun and occasional profit. Others pursue their magical studies with deadly seriousness, seeking the power of the gods to make and destroy worlds.

2.name: Transmutation Savant
2.desc: The gold and time you must spend to copy a transmutation spell into your spellbook is halved.

2.name: Minor Alchemy
2.desc: You may temporarily alter the physical properties of one nonmagical object, changing it from one substance into another. You perform a special alchemical procedure on one object composed entirely of wood, stone (but not a gemstone), iron, copper, or silver, transforming it into a different one of those materials. For each 10 minutes you spend performing the procedure, you can transform up to 1cu.ft. of material. After 1 hour, or until you lose your concentration (as if you were concentrating on a spell), the material reverts to its original substance.

6.name: Transmuter's Stone
6.desc: You may spend 8 hours creating a transmuter's stone that stores transmutation magic. You may benefit from the stone yourself, or give it to another creature. A creature gains a benefit of your choice as long as the stone is in the creature's possession. Whenever you creature When you create the stone, choose the benefit from the following options:

content.desc: Darkvision out to a range of 60ft.
An increase to speed of 10ft. while the creature is unencumbered.
Proficiency in CON saving throws.
Resistance to @acid, @cold, @fire, @lightning, or @thunder damage, your choice.

10.name: Shapechanger
10.desc: You may add the @polymorph spell to your spellbook, if it is not there already. You may cast the @polymorph spell without spending a spell slot; you may only target yourself, and transform into a beast of a CR of at most 1. You may use this feature once per short rest, though you are able to cast the @polymorph spell with a spell slot normally.

14.name: Master Transmuter
14.desc: As an action, you may consume the reserve of transmutation magic stored within your transmuter's stone in a single burst. Whenever you do so, the stone is destroyed and cannot be remade until you finish a long rest, but you may choose one of the following effects:

content.name: Major Transformation
content.desc: You may transmute one nonmagical object no larger than a 5ft. cube into another nonmagical object of similar size and mass, and of equal or lesser value. You must spend 10 minutes handling the object to transform it.

content.name: Panacea
content.desc: You remove all curses, diseases and poisons affecting a creature that you touch with the transmuter's stone. The creature regains all its hitpoints.

content.name: Restore Life
content.desc: You cast the @raise dead spell on a creature you touch with the transmuter's stone, without expending a spell slot or needing to have the spell in your spellbook.

content.name: Restore Youth
content.desc: You reduce the apparent age of a willing creature you touch with the transmuter's stone by 3d10 years, to a minimum of 13 years. This effect does not extend the creature's lifespan.
